Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First all cases start with RA right Case 1 2 3 all start with that Then after RA comes VF then AE

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets confirm case 2 RA VF AE yes thats the start Case 1 same case 3 same

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 1 after AE they did RMI then VF again then AD Case 3 AE RMI RMI VF then AD

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: So after AE theres an optional loop where you can request missing info any number of times

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Wait after RMI comes VF right Case 1 after AE is RMI then VF Case 3 AE RMI RMI VF

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Oh no earlier I thought after RMI comes VF but in case 3 there are two RMIs in a row before VF So my earlier assumption was wrong

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets list the traces Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After AE there are two possibilities directly go to AD or do one or more RMIs followed by a VF then go to AD

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Option a Directly go to AD Option b Do one or more RMIs followed by VF then go to AD Case 2 takes option a Case 1 takes option b with one RMI Case 3 takes option b with two RMIs

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Then after AD comes SC then AC All cases have that no exceptions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the entire process starts with RA then VF then AE Then after AE there is a choice Then after AD there is SC then AC So the root should be a sequence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Option b is one or more RMIs followed by VF So that can be modeled as a loop with RMI as A and tau as B giving one or more RMIs then VF

Name: Validation - PE
Text: If A is RMI and B is tau then first execute RMI then either exit or do tau and RMI again That gives one two three or more RMIs exactly what we need

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: After AE we can have an XOR between tau and a sequence of the RMI loop followed by VF Then after that proceed to AD

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 choose tau then AD Case 1 choose the second branch loop once then VF then AD Case 3 choose the second branch loop twice then VF then AD

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: No parallel operators are needed here because all activities are in a fixed order except the RMI repetition

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This model does not allow RMI without VF and does not allow VF after AE without at least one RMI which matches the log

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: If the loop were on RMI followed by VF then it would allow RMI VF RMI VF which is not observed in the log so the loop should be only on RMI

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Allowing any number of RMIs is a simple generalization that balances fitness precision and simplicity better than restricting only to one or two repetitions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the final process tree is a sequence of initial sequence RA VF AE then an XOR of tau and sequence of loop RMI tau then VF then final sequence AD SC AC
